        "7. The Singapore AI Advantage - Leadership/Tech (Score: 8/10)"
    ],
    "headline": "Step 3 Part A: Saving topics to file",
    "tool_name": "text_editor:write",
    "tool_args": {
        "path": "/a0/usr/workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_2026-04-08.md",
        "content": "# Blog Topic Ideas - 2026-04-08

## 1. The Trust Paradox: Why 84% of Developers Use AI But Only 29% Trust It (And How We Got to 100%)
- **Pillar:** Leadership
- **Score:** 9/10
- **Angle:** Michael drove department-wide AI adoption from 28% to 100%, giving him unique data on overcoming trust barriers. This post will contrast the industry's "adoption-trust paradox" (Stack Overflow survey) with concrete tactics that worked in a large retail organization, including code review guardrails, gradual rollout strategies, and psychological safety frameworks.
- **Trending Signal:** Stack Overflow survey of 49,000 developers reveals 84% use AI but only 29% trust it; McKinsey's "State of AI Trust in 2026" highlights the critical gap between adoption and confidence. The Straits Times reports Singapore firms advancing AI adoption with regional leadership status.
- **Related Expertise:** AI adoption at organizational scale (drove 28% to 100% department-wide), Team leadership (up to 14 cross-functional members), Engineering culture

## 2. From Pilot Purgatory to Production Reality: What the 11% Getting Multi-Agent Systems Right Actually Did Differently
- **Pillar:** Technology
- **Score:** 8/10
- **Angle:** Gartner forecasts 40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by end-2026, yet only 11% are in actual production. Michael built 2 production multi-agent systems (enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report), providing hands-on lessons on guardrails, failure modes, and operational patterns that generic tutorials miss.
- **Trending Signal:** Gartner's "Agentic AI at Scale" report shows the 11% production reality vs. 40% forecast; MCP Dev Summit NYC (March 2026) featured 46 organizations discussing scaling challenges; LinkedIn article "Agent-to-Agent is an Anti-Pattern" highlights common pitfalls.
- **Related Expertise:** Multi-agent orchestration (built 2 production systems), Agent Zero framework (runs 11 specialist agents), Prompt engineering and context engineering for production agentic systems

## 3. Beyond ROCm: The Strix Halo Optimization Stack That Finally Beats AMD's Own Implementation for Local LLMs
- **Pillar:** Technology
- **Score:** 7/10
- **Angle:** With cpp now outperforming ROCm on Strix Halo, Michael can share his hands-on benchmarking of a 128GB unified memory AI server. This post covers practical optimization techniques (memory allocation, quantization strategies, backend selection) that generic guides miss, plus power consumption trade-offs from his homelab migration experience.
- **Trending Signal:** LinkedIn article "Running 122B-Parameter LLMs Locally on AMD Strix Halo" documents recent optimizations; Hardware Corner net benchmarks show cpp outperforming ROCm; Reddit thread discusses January 2026 stable configurations for ROCm+Linux on Strix Halo.
- **Related Expertise:** AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server (local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp), Homelab migration (30% power reduction), Cost optimization

## 4. MCP at Scale: Real-World Lessons from 46 Organizations on Context Bloat, Security Gaps, and Integration Patterns
- **Pillar:** Technology
- **Score:** 9/10
- **Angle:** Michael's Agent Zero skills for Gmail/Jira/GChat via CDP browser give him direct experience solving MCP's biggest production challenges: context bloat, synchronization issues, and security. This post translates lessons from the MCP Dev Summit NYC (March 2026) into actionable patterns for enterprise deployment.
- **Trending Signal:** MCP Dev Summit NYC (March 2026) featured 46 organizations reporting context bloat and scaling challenges; The New Stack's "MCP Roadmap 2026" details interoperability standards; AI Business article "MCP is Alive, but Faces Challenges" highlights April 2026 production realities.
- **Related Expertise:** Agent Zero framework (skills for Gmail/Jira/GChat via CDP browser), MCP integration experience, Vendor evaluation expertise

## 5. The DORA Metrics Blind Spot: Why AI-Coded PRs Take 91% Longer to Review (And How We Fixed It)
- **Pillar:** Technology/Leadership
- **Score:** 8/10
- **Angle:** AI delivers 55% faster task completion but increases PR review times by 91% and bug-fix volume. Michael's DORA metrics experience (<10d cycle time, CFR <2%, SLO >98%) provides data-driven solutions for measuring true AI ROI beyond surface-level productivity gains.
- **Trending Signal:** Exceeds.ai report "Engineering Effectiveness in 2026" reveals the 55% faster task completion vs. 91% longer review time paradox; InfoQ's March 2026 DORA report highlights AI amplification challenges; Oobeya.io article "DORA Metrics Are Not Enough in 2026" discusses elite team practices.
- **Related Expertise:** DORA metrics, SLO management, engineering culture (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%), AI adoption at organizational scale

## 6. AI Intensifies Work, Doesn't Reduce It: A Practical Framework for Preventing Burnout in the Agentic Era
- **Pillar:** Life
- **Score:** 7/10
- **Angle:** ActivTrak's 2026 State of the Workplace report shows AI intensifying work (not reducing it) while only 39% report productivity gains. Michael's GPS Framework experience and hands-on agent orchestration provide a practical approach to managing increased cognitive load without burnout.
- **Trending Signal:** ActivTrak Productivity Lab "State of the Workplace" report (April 2026); Harvard Business Review article "AI Doesn't Reduce Work—It Intensifies It" (February 2026); BCG study showing AI reshapes more jobs than it replaces.
- **Related Expertise:** The GPS Framework for Productivity (published post, but this extends with new data), Multi-agent orchestration (managing cognitive load), Team leadership

## 7. The Singapore AI Advantage: How 81% of SEA Companies Are Scaling Beyond Pilots (And What You Can Steal)
- **Pillar:** Leadership/Tech
- **Score:** 8/10
- **Angle:** McKinsey-EDB-Tech in Asia report shows SEA AI adoption outpacing global average (81% piloting/scaling). Michael's experience leading AI transformation at a large retail organization plus Singapore context provides unique insights into scaling strategies that work in APAC markets.
- **Trending Signal:** McKinsey-EDB-Tech in Asia report "AI adoption in Southeast Asia is outpacing the global average" (March 2026); The Straits Times reports Singapore firms advancing AI adoption; Business Times article on SEA companies scaling AI-powered projects.
- **Related Expertise:** AI adoption at organizational scale, Retail media networks/ad-tech experience ($1M to $10M+ GMV), Team leadership in APAC context"
    }
}

## 1. TITLE: The Trust Paradox: Why 84% of Developers Use AI But Only 29% Trust It (And How We Got to 100%)
**ANGLE:** Michael drove department-wide AI adoption from 28% to 100%, giving him unique data on overcoming trust barriers. This post will contrast the industry's "adoption-trust paradox" (Stack Overflow survey) with concrete tactics that worked in a large retail organization, including code review guardrails, gradual rollout strategies, and psychological safety frameworks.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Stack Overflow survey of 49,000 developers reveals 84% use AI but only 29% trust it; McKinsey's "State of AI Trust in 2026" highlights the critical gap between adoption and confidence. The Straits Times reports Singapore firms advancing AI adoption with regional leadership status.

**SCORE:** 9/10

---

## 2. TITLE: From Pilot Purgatory to Production Reality: What the 11% Getting Multi-Agent Systems Right Actually Did Differently
**ANGLE:** Gartner forecasts 40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by end-2026, yet only 11% are in actual production. Michael built 2 production multi-agent systems (enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report), providing hands-on lessons on guardrails, failure modes, and operational patterns that generic tutorials miss.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Gartner's "Agentic AI at Scale" report shows the 11% production reality vs. 40% forecast; MCP Dev Summit NYC (March 2026) featured 46 organizations discussing scaling challenges; LinkedIn article "Agent-to-Agent is an Anti-Pattern" highlights common pitfalls.

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 3. TITLE: Beyond ROCm: The Strix Halo Optimization Stack That Finally Beats AMD's Own Implementation for Local LLMs
**ANGLE:** With cpp now outperforming ROCm on Strix Halo, Michael can share his hands-on benchmarking of a 128GB unified memory AI server.
